settings.description: IP address to bind to. Use `0.0.0.0` to serve to your local network.
settings.name: host

settings.description: Port to bind to.
settings.name: port

settings.description: Allowed CORS origins.
settings.name: allow_origins

settings.description: Allow CORS credentials.
settings.name: allow_credentials

settings.description: Methods allowed for CORS.
settings.name: allow_methods

settings.description: Headers allowed for CORS.
settings.name: allow_headers

settings.description: SSL certificate file for HTTPS. See https://www.uvicorn.org/settings/#https.
settings.name: ssl_certfile

settings.description: SSL key file for HTTPS. See https://www.uvicorn.org/settings/#https.
settings.name: ssl_keyfile

settings.description: Enable logging of parsed prompt tokens.
settings.name: log_tokenization

settings.description: Enable patchmatch inpaint code.
settings.name: patchmatch

settings.description: Path to the models directory.
settings.name: models_dir

settings.description: Path to the converted models cache directory (DEPRECATED, but do not delete because it is needed for migration from previous versions).
settings.name: convert_cache_dir

settings.description: Path to the directory that contains dynamically downloaded models.
settings.name: download_cache_dir

settings.description: Path to directory of legacy checkpoint config files.
settings.name: legacy_conf_dir

settings.description: Path to InvokeAI databases directory.
settings.name: db_dir

settings.description: Path to directory for outputs.
settings.name: outputs_dir

settings.description: Path to directory for custom nodes.
settings.name: custom_nodes_dir

settings.description: Path to directory for style presets.
settings.name: style_presets_dir

settings.description: Path to directory for workflow thumbnails.
settings.name: workflow_thumbnails_dir

settings.description: Log handler. Valid options are "console", "file=<path>", "syslog=path|address:host:port", "http=<url>".
settings.name: log_handlers

settings.description: Log format. Use "plain" for text-only, "color" for colorized output, "legacy" for 2.3-style logging and "syslog" for syslog-style.
settings.name: log_format

settings.description: Emit logging messages at this level or higher.
settings.name: log_level

settings.description: Log SQL queries. `log_level` must be `debug` for this to do anything. Extremely verbose.
settings.name: log_sql

settings.description: Log level for network-related messages. 'info' and 'debug' are very verbose.
settings.name: log_level_network

settings.description: Use in-memory database. Useful for development.
settings.name: use_memory_db

settings.description: Automatically reload when Python sources are changed. Does not reload node definitions.
settings.name: dev_reload

settings.description: Enable graph profiling using `cProfile`.
settings.name: profile_graphs

settings.description: An optional prefix for profile output files.
settings.name: profile_prefix

settings.description: Path to profiles output directory.
settings.name: profiles_dir

settings.description: The maximum amount of CPU RAM to use for model caching in GB. If unset, the limit will be configured based on the available RAM. In most cases, it is recommended to leave this unset.
settings.name: max_cache_ram_gb

settings.description: The amount of VRAM to use for model caching in GB. If unset, the limit will be configured based on the available VRAM and the device_working_mem_gb. In most cases, it is recommended to leave this unset.
settings.name: max_cache_vram_gb

settings.description: If True, a memory snapshot will be captured before and after every model cache operation, and the result will be logged (at debug level). There is a time cost to capturing the memory snapshots, so it is recommended to only enable this feature if you are actively inspecting the model cache's behaviour.
settings.name: log_memory_usage

settings.description: How long to keep models in cache after last use, in minutes. A value of 0 (the default) means models are kept in cache indefinitely. If no model generations occur within the timeout period, the model cache is cleared using the same logic as the 'Clear Model Cache' button.
settings.name: model_cache_keep_alive_min

settings.description: The amount of working memory to keep available on the compute device (in GB). Has no effect if running on CPU. If you are experiencing OOM errors, try increasing this value.
settings.name: device_working_mem_gb

settings.description: Enable partial loading of models. This enables models to run with reduced VRAM requirements (at the cost of slower speed) by streaming the model from RAM to VRAM as its used. In some edge cases, partial loading can cause models to run more slowly if they were previously being fully loaded into VRAM.
settings.name: enable_partial_loading

settings.description: Whether to keep a full RAM copy of a model's weights when the model is loaded in VRAM. Keeping a RAM copy increases average RAM usage, but speeds up model switching and LoRA patching (assuming there is sufficient RAM). Set this to False if RAM pressure is consistently high.
settings.name: keep_ram_copy_of_weights

settings.description: DEPRECATED: This setting is no longer used. It has been replaced by `max_cache_ram_gb`, but most users will not need to use this config since automatic cache size limits should work well in most cases. This config setting will be removed once the new model cache behavior is stable.
settings.name: ram

settings.description: DEPRECATED: This setting is no longer used. It has been replaced by `max_cache_vram_gb`, but most users will not need to use this config since automatic cache size limits should work well in most cases. This config setting will be removed once the new model cache behavior is stable.
settings.name: vram

settings.description: DEPRECATED: This setting is no longer used. Lazy-offloading is enabled by default. This config setting will be removed once the new model cache behavior is stable.
settings.name: lazy_offload

settings.description: Configure the Torch CUDA memory allocator. This will impact peak reserved VRAM usage and performance. Setting to "backend:cudaMallocAsync" works well on many systems. The optimal configuration is highly dependent on the system configuration (device type, VRAM, CUDA driver version, etc.), so must be tuned experimentally.
settings.name: pytorch_cuda_alloc_conf

settings.description:
Preferred execution device. `auto` will choose the device depending on the hardware platform and the installed torch capabilities.
Valid values: `auto`, `cpu`, `cuda`, `mps`, `cuda:N` (where N is a device number)
settings.name: device

settings.description: Floating point precision. `float16` will consume half the memory of `float32` but produce slightly lower-quality images. The `auto` setting will guess the proper precision based on your video card and operating system.
settings.name: precision

settings.description: Whether to calculate guidance in serial instead of in parallel, lowering memory requirements.
settings.name: sequential_guidance

settings.description: Attention type.
settings.name: attention_type

settings.description: Slice size, valid when attention_type=="sliced".
settings.name: attention_slice_size

settings.description: Whether to enable tiled VAE decode (reduces memory consumption with some performance penalty).
settings.name: force_tiled_decode

settings.description: The compress_level setting of PIL.Image.save(), used for PNG encoding. All settings are lossless. 0 = no compression, 1 = fastest with slightly larger filesize, 9 = slowest with smallest filesize. 1 is typically the best setting.
settings.name: pil_compress_level

settings.description: Maximum number of items in the session queue.
settings.name: max_queue_size

settings.description: Empties session queue on startup. If true, disables `max_queue_history`.
settings.name: clear_queue_on_startup

settings.description: Keep the last N completed, failed, and canceled queue items. Older items are deleted on startup. Set to 0 to prune all terminal items. Ignored if `clear_queue_on_startup` is true.
settings.name: max_queue_history

settings.description: List of nodes to allow. Omit to allow all.
settings.name: allow_nodes

settings.description: List of nodes to deny. Omit to deny none.
settings.name: deny_nodes

settings.description: How many cached nodes to keep in memory.
settings.name: node_cache_size

settings.description: Model hashing algorthim for model installs. 'blake3_multi' is best for SSDs. 'blake3_single' is best for spinning disk HDDs. 'random' disables hashing, instead assigning a UUID to models. Useful when using a memory db to reduce model installation time, or if you don't care about storing stable hashes for models. Alternatively, any other hashlib algorithm is accepted, though these are not nearly as performant as blake3.
settings.name: hashing_algorithm

settings.description: List of regular expression and token pairs used when downloading models from URLs. The download URL is tested against the regex, and if it matches, the token is provided in as a Bearer token.
settings.name: remote_api_tokens

settings.description: Scan the models directory on startup, registering orphaned models. This is typically only used in conjunction with `use_memory_db` for testing purposes.
settings.name: scan_models_on_startup

settings.description: UNSAFE. Disable the picklescan security check during model installation. Recommended only for development and testing purposes. This will allow arbitrary code execution during model installation, so should never be used in production.
settings.name: unsafe_disable_picklescan

settings.description: Allow installation of models that we are unable to identify. If enabled, models will be marked as `unknown` in the database, and will not have any metadata associated with them. If disabled, unknown models will be rejected during installation.
settings.name: allow_unknown_models

settings.description: Enable multiuser support. When disabled, the application runs in single-user mode using a default system account with administrator privileges. When enabled, requires user authentication and authorization.
settings.name: multiuser

settings.description: Enforce strict password requirements. When True, passwords must contain uppercase, lowercase, and numbers. When False (default), any password is accepted but its strength (weak/moderate/strong) is reported to the user.
settings.name: strict_password_checking
